title: The Shape of a Sign
subtitle: Symbols, coordinates, centering, and the signbox
summary: Canonical statement of symbols, coordinates, centering, and signbox structure.
synopsis: A Formal SignWriting word is not complete until its symbols, coordinates, signbox behavior, tight bounding boxes, and centering logic are understood. This entry states the spatial layer in its smallest stable form and shows how the signbox works as a spatial arrangement carried in text.

slides.title: The Signbox Is a Spatial Arrangement
slides.summary: The signbox is where Formal SignWriting becomes visibly spatial and technically precise.

image_prompts.concrete: Technical diagram of a SignWriting signbox with symbols arranged spatially and labeled as text plus spatial arrangement, scholarly and clean
image_prompts.abstract: A spatial field of symbols forming one coherent written object

bullets: The sign as an arranged object
slides.speech: This entry states the spatial layer of the model. Formal SignWriting is not only a grammar of symbols. It also has to preserve where those symbols are placed, how far they extend, and how they are normalized. The signbox becomes understandable once those pieces are kept in view together, and that spatial layer remains stable even as rendering and implementation continue to grow around it.
slides.notes: Use this as the spatial counterpart to the opening deck.

slides.title: Symbol Classes Define Technical Roles
slides.summary: Symbol classes matter because they support different jobs in writing, centering, search, and rendering.

image_prompts.concrete: Scholarly classification slide showing grouped SignWriting symbol families with labels for writing, location, and punctuation, clean white background
image_prompts.abstract: Distinct families of symbols organizing themselves into technical roles

slides.speech: Symbol classes are not only a catalog. Different symbol ranges support different technical behavior. Some belong directly in the spatial signbox, some help with sequence and location handling, and some matter especially for centering, rendering, or punctuation. The spatial layer becomes much more legible once those roles are named instead of treated as a flat inventory.
slides.notes: This is the right place to make symbol classes feel architectural.

slides.title: Coordinates Turn Space Into Data
slides.summary: Coordinates, tight bounding boxes, and maximum coordinates make the signbox usable in software.

image_prompts.concrete: Technical diagram of a SignWriting signbox with coordinate labels, tight bounding boxes, and axis-style annotations, precise and academic
image_prompts.abstract: Spatial placement becoming measurable and computational

slides.speech: Coordinates are not incidental metadata. They are the mechanism that makes spatial relation usable in software. Each placed symbol gives a top-left coordinate, while preprocessing supplies the symbol size needed to build a tight bounding box and determine maximum coordinates. That is how the signbox becomes usable for layout, sizing, rendering, and later search workflows. Visible arrangement becomes stable technical structure only once those calculations are explicit.
slides.notes: This slide should connect the visual arrangement to practical software behavior.

slides.title: Centering Makes Signs Travel Well
slides.summary: Normalization is one of the strongest technical moves in the system.
slides.speech: Centering is one of the strongest technical moves in Formal SignWriting. Horizontal centering uses `hcenter` symbols when available, and vertical centering uses `vcenter` symbols when available, with fallback to all symbols when those ranges are absent. The sign is temporarily expanded into tight bounding boxes, the midpoint is calculated from the relevant extents, and all coordinates are shifted together. Without that normalization, too much of the system becomes fragile or inconsistent. With it, the spatial layer becomes much more consistent across search, rendering, and implementation.
slides.notes: This is where the deck should start pointing forward to search, rendering, and implementation.

image_prompts.concrete: Technical signbox slide showing an unnormalized sign centered to 500x500 and then reused for stable layout, search, and rendering
image_prompts.abstract: A spatial sign recentered so it can travel reliably across systems
